methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Neko Health is a Series B biotech and health company founded by Daniel Ek and Hjalmar Nilsonne, currently valued at $1.8B post-money on $326M+ raised. The valuation analysis employs a comp universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, alongside 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage advancement, implying $3.4B. Peer-median analysis across four same-stage, same-sector companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Chai Discovery ($1.3B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as target revenue is not disclosed; the sector P/S median across public peers is 6.7x, but without revenue data this method is inert.

The triangulated valuation range is $892M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $2.2B. The current $1.8B post-money sits near the peer median, suggesting the company is priced appropriately relative to same-stage cohorts but below stage-progression expectations. Key uncertainties include revenue and unit economics not provided in available data, which would refine public-comp anchoring and inform growth trajectory assumptions underlying the stage-progression multiple.
